summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain with radiation to the neck.

History of Present Illness
The patient reports onset of right elbow pain approximately one day ago, with no specific injury. He describes the pain as aching and heavy, extending up the arm to the neck. He denies recent trauma. He reports taking motrin for the pain, which provides minimal relief. He mowed the grass yesterday and mentions some discomfort, but no specific injury. He has been doing some household chores. He denies any numbness or weakness. He reports no prior similar episodes.

Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation over the distal right elbow and upper arm. Range of motion is limited by pain. Blood pressure is 150/90 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm, regular. A slight murmur is auscultated. Glucose level is pending. EKG is ordered.

Assessment
Right elbow pain with radiation to the neck, potentially musculoskeletal in origin. Rule out cardiac etiology given chest pain and associated arm pain.

Plan
Order an electrocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac ischemia. Administer aspirin and nitroglycerin as ordered. Obtain a glucose test. Continue metformin at 500 mg twice daily. Schedule follow-up in 24-48 hours to assess response to treatment and further evaluate. Refer to cardiology for further evaluation if EKG is abnormal.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Left arm pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Zachary Wood is a 45-year-old right-hand-dominant male who presents to the clinic today for the evaluation of left arm pain. The onset of his pain began yesterday when he woke up with pain. He denies any specific injury. The patient locates his pain from his left elbow up all the way to his neck. He describes his pain as an achiness. The patient has been taking Motrin for pain, which does not provide him with relief. He also reports intermittent chest pain that radiates up his neck. He notes that he had to stop cutting the grass the other day because of chest pain. The patient denies any shortness of breath.

For his hypertension, the patient states that his blood pressure has been around 125/72 recently. He is still taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

Regarding his type 2 diabetes, his last hemoglobin A1c was 7.2. He takes metformin 500 mg. The patient states that he does not check his blood glucose levels regularly.

MEDICAL HISTORY

Patient reports that he has a past medical history of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

MEDICATIONS

Patient reports that he currently takes metformin 500 mg and Norvasc 2.5 mg.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Cardiovascular: Reports chest pain.
Respiratory: Denies shortness of breath.
Musculoskeletal: Reports left elbow, arm, and neck pain.

VITALS

The patient's blood pressure today is 150/90.

PHYSICAL EXAM

CV: Slight murmur detected, not heard before.

ASSESSMENT

Left arm pain.
Type 2 diabetes.
Hypertension.

PLAN

Regarding his left arm pain, I have explained to him that his symptoms are likely a mild heart attack. I have recommended that we obtain an EKG for further evaluation. I have also recommended that the patient take aspirin and nitroglycerin for pain. The patient will follow up with me once the EKG results are available for review and further discussion.

In regards to his diabetes, the patient will continue the 500 mg of metformin for now.

For his hypertension, the patient will continue the 2.5 mg of Norvasc.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right elbow pain with radiation to the neck.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports onset of right elbow pain approximately one day ago, with no specific injury. He describes the pain as aching and heavy, extending up the arm to the neck. He denies recent trauma. He reports taking motrin for the pain, which provides minimal relief. He mowed the grass yesterday and mentions some discomfort, but no specific injury. He has been doing some household chores. He denies any numbness or weakness. He reports no prior similar episodes.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation over the distal right elbow and upper arm. Range of motion is limited by pain. Blood pressure is 150/90 mmHg. Heart rate is 72 bpm, regular. A slight murmur is auscultated. Glucose level is pending. EKG is ordered.
Assessment
Right elbow pain with radiation to the neck, potentially musculoskeletal in origin. Rule out cardiac etiology given chest pain and associated arm pain.
Plan
Order an electrocardiogram to evaluate for cardiac ischemia. Administer aspirin and nitroglycerin as ordered. Obtain a glucose test. Continue metformin at 500 mg twice daily. Schedule follow-up in 24-48 hours to assess response to treatment and further evaluate. Refer to cardiology for further evaluation if EKG is abnormal.

section_miscategorizations.text: Rule out cardiac etiology given chest pain and associated arm pain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The note never documents chest pain in the History of Present Illness, yet this sentence introduces 'chest pain and associated arm pain' as if it were part of the clinical picture. In the Assessment, this appears as a new symptom rather than a diagnostic synthesis of previously documented history, which could mislead the reader about what the patient actually reported.

A pending lab is more appropriately documented as an ordered test in the Plan. Listing it under Findings implies a result is already available, though this is a modest organizational issue rather than substantially misleading.

Test orders belong in the Plan. Placing the EKG order in Findings blurs the distinction between what has been found versus what is being done, which can mislead a reader about whether an EKG result is available.
